summary: Conceptual demonstration of signature cache poisoning vulnerability. Cache keys use only signature bytes without public key or message hash, creating potential for collisions and bypass.
expected_security_impact: Cache collisions could allow votes with same signature bytes to bypass verification.
actual_observed_result: Code review confirms signature-only cache keys. Conceptual PoC demonstrates attack scenario. Full PoC requires demonstrating actual cache collision.
verification_method: Code review confirms cache key construction uses only signature bytes. The conceptual PoC explains the poisoning mechanism.
evidence_produced_by_run: Cache key construction identified at types/validation.go:323-326
Attack scenario explained for cache poisoning
limitations_or_blockers: Full PoC requires demonstrating actual cache collision with two votes having same signature
Additional checks (address, vote bytes) provide partial protection
Exploit probability is low but design is fundamentally flawed

reproduction.source_acquisition: Conceptual demonstration based on code review of CometBFT repository
